michaelryan.background

Class TaskCompleter<T>

  • java.lang.Object
    • michaelryan.background.TaskCompleter<T>
  • Type Parameters:
    T - the return type of the task
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    Runnable
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    FactorialComputer, Sleeper

    public abstract class TaskCompleter<T>
extends Object
implements Runnable
    A class that provides a framework for performing a task in the background. If the result of the task is requested and computation has not finished, the calling thread "spins" until computation is complete.

    This class must be extended, with the doTask() method providing the implementation of the task. Such implementation can save a created object if required, and retrieved with getResult()

    The only methods to be used by a calling thread are begin() and getResult().

      private boolean taskIsComplete

      void begin()
      Begins execution of the task.
      protected abstract T doTask()
      Implements the task to execute.
      T getResult()
      Returns the result of the task.
      void run()
      When an object implementing interface Runnable is used to create a thread, starting the thread causes the object's run method to be called in that separately executing thread.

    • Method Detail

      • getResult

        public T getResult()
        Returns the result of the task.
        Returns:
        the result of the task

      • run

        public void run()
        Description copied from interface: java.lang.Runnable
        When an object implementing interface Runnable is used to create a thread, starting the thread causes the object's run method to be called in that separately executing thread.

        The general contract of the method run is that it may take any action whatsoever.

        Specified by:
        run in interface Runnable
        See Also:
        Thread.run()

      • doTask

        protected abstract T doTask()
        Implements the task to execute. Must return the result of the computation, or null if not applicable
        Returns:
        the result of the task, or null if not applicable

      • begin

        public void begin()
        Begins execution of the task.
